title: Bull Bitcoin Secures MiCA License in France, Preserving Full Self-Custody and Privacy Features
summary: Bull Bitcoin has obtained a MiCA license in France while maintaining full self-custody and privacy features. Founder Francis Pouliot announced the self-financed, three-year achievement, highlighting successful PASSI and DORA cybersecurity audits without outsourcing core infrastructure.
body:
Bull Bitcoin, a Canadian Bitcoin exchange, has successfully secured a MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets) license in France. The license allows the company to operate legally within the European Union while preserving its commitment to self-custody and privacy. This achievement is notable because it was self-financed and took three years to complete.

The company passed both PASSI and DORA cybersecurity audits, demonstrating its robust security measures. Founder Francis Pouliot emphasized that Bull Bitcoin did not outsource any core infrastructure, ensuring full control over its operations. This license is a significant milestone as it allows Bull Bitcoin to offer its services to European customers while maintaining its core principles.

For everyday users, this means they can continue to enjoy the benefits of self-custody and privacy when using Bull Bitcoin's services. The MiCA license provides regulatory clarity and protection, making it a safer option for those concerned about compliance and security. Bull Bitcoin's commitment to not outsourcing core infrastructure also ensures that user data and funds remain secure.

This development is particularly important for Bitcoin enthusiasts who value self-custody and privacy. Bull Bitcoin's ability to secure a MiCA license while maintaining these features sets a precedent for other exchanges. Users should watch for any updates on Bull Bitcoin's services in Europe and how this license might influence other exchanges to adopt similar practices.
twitterThread: Bull Bitcoin just secured a MiCA license in France—while keeping full self-custody and privacy. A rare win for user control in regulated crypto.
It took 3 years, self-financing, and passing tough PASSI and DORA cybersecurity audits. No outsourcing core infrastructure. That's how you do compliance right.

If you care about self-custody, watch how this plays out. Bull Bitcoin might just set a new standard. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-23-bull-bitcoin-secures-mica-license-in-france-keeps-self-custody
standaloneTweet: Bull Bitcoin secured a MiCA license in France—without giving up self-custody or privacy. A big win for users who want control over their crypto.
